---
title: "Multi-Criteria Evaluation for Optimal K"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
embeddings <- as.matrix(read.csv("data/embeddings.csv", header = FALSE))
unique_values <- readLines("data/unique_values.txt")
rownames(embeddings) <- unique_values

X <- embeddings
```

```{r}
library(movMF)
library(mclust)
library(cluster)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# ============================================
# 1. COMPREHENSIVE K SELECTION FUNCTION
# ============================================

find_optimal_k <- function(X, k_range = 2:15, methods = c("vmf", "gmm", "fcm")) {
  
  # Normalize data
  X_normalized <- X / sqrt(rowSums(X^2))
  
  # Storage for results
  results <- data.frame()
  
  cat("Testing k from", min(k_range), "to", max(k_range), "...\n")
  
  for (k in k_range) {
    cat(sprintf("k = %d... ", k))
    
    # Cosine distance matrix (compute once per k)
    cos_dist <- as.dist(1 - X_normalized %*% t(X_normalized))
    
    # ---------------------
    # vMF Mixture Model
    # ---------------------
    if ("vmf" %in% methods) {
      vmf_model <- movMF(X, k = k, nruns = 10)
      vmf_clusters <- predict(vmf_model, X)
      vmf_probs <- predict(vmf_model, X, type = "memberships")
      
      # Metrics
      vmf_sil <- mean(silhouette(vmf_clusters, cos_dist)[, 3])
      vmf_loglik <- vmf_model$L
      vmf_bic <- -2 * vmf_loglik + (k * ncol(X) + k - 1) * log(nrow(X))
      vmf_aic <- -2 * vmf_loglik + 2 * (k * ncol(X) + k - 1)
      vmf_entropy <- -mean(rowSums(vmf_probs * log(vmf_probs + 1e-10)))
      vmf_ch <- calinski_harabasz(X_normalized, vmf_clusters)
      vmf_db <- davies_bouldin(X_normalized, vmf_clusters)
      
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
        k = k, method = "vMF",
        silhouette = vmf_sil,
        loglik = vmf_loglik,
        BIC = vmf_bic,
        AIC = vmf_aic,
        entropy = vmf_entropy,
        calinski_harabasz = vmf_ch,
        davies_bouldin = vmf_db
      ))
    }
    
    # ---------------------
    # GMM
    # ---------------------
    if ("gmm" %in% methods) {
      gmm_model <- Mclust(X_normalized, G = k, modelNames = "VVI", verbose = FALSE)
      gmm_clusters <- gmm_model$classification
      gmm_probs <- gmm_model$z
      
      gmm_sil <- mean(silhouette(gmm_clusters, cos_dist)[, 3])
      gmm_loglik <- gmm_model$loglik
      gmm_bic <- gmm_model$bic
      gmm_aic <- -2 * gmm_loglik + 2 * gmm_model$df
      gmm_entropy <- -mean(rowSums(gmm_probs * log(gmm_probs + 1e-10)))
      gmm_ch <- calinski_harabasz(X_normalized, gmm_clusters)
      gmm_db <- davies_bouldin(X_normalized, gmm_clusters)
      
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
        k = k, method = "GMM",
        silhouette = gmm_sil,
        loglik = gmm_loglik,
        BIC = gmm_bic,
        AIC = gmm_aic,
        entropy = gmm_entropy,
        calinski_harabasz = gmm_ch,
        davies_bouldin = gmm_db
      ))
    }
    
    # ---------------------
    # FCM
    # ---------------------
    if ("fcm" %in% methods) {
      library(e1071)
      fcm_model <- cmeans(X_normalized, centers = k, iter.max = 100, m = 2)
      fcm_clusters <- fcm_model$cluster
      fcm_probs <- fcm_model$membership
      
      fcm_sil <- mean(silhouette(fcm_clusters, cos_dist)[, 3])
      fcm_entropy <- -mean(rowSums(fcm_probs * log(fcm_probs + 1e-10)))
      fcm_ch <- calinski_harabasz(X_normalized, fcm_clusters)
      fcm_db <- davies_bouldin(X_normalized, fcm_clusters)
      
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
        k = k, method = "FCM",
        silhouette = fcm_sil,
        loglik = NA,
        BIC = NA,
        AIC = NA,
        entropy = fcm_entropy,
        calinski_harabasz = fcm_ch,
        davies_bouldin = fcm_db
      ))
    }
    
    cat("done\n")
  }
  
  return(results)
}

# Helper functions for metrics
calinski_harabasz <- function(X, clusters) {
  # Calinski-Harabasz index (higher is better)
  n <- nrow(X)
  k <- length(unique(clusters))
  
  overall_mean <- colMeans(X)
  
  # Between-cluster variance
  BGSS <- 0
  for (i in unique(clusters)) {
    cluster_points <- X[clusters == i, , drop = FALSE]
    n_i <- nrow(cluster_points)
    cluster_mean <- colMeans(cluster_points)
    BGSS <- BGSS + n_i * sum((cluster_mean - overall_mean)^2)
  }
  
  # Within-cluster variance
  WGSS <- 0
  for (i in unique(clusters)) {
    cluster_points <- X[clusters == i, , drop = FALSE]
    cluster_mean <- colMeans(cluster_points)
    WGSS <- WGSS + sum(apply(cluster_points, 1, function(x) sum((x - cluster_mean)^2)))
  }
  
  CH <- (BGSS / (k - 1)) / (WGSS / (n - k))
  return(CH)
}

davies_bouldin <- function(X, clusters) {
  # Davies-Bouldin index (lower is better)
  k <- length(unique(clusters))
  cluster_ids <- unique(clusters)
  
  # Calculate centroids and dispersions
  centroids <- matrix(0, nrow = k, ncol = ncol(X))
  dispersions <- numeric(k)
  
  for (i in 1:k) {
    cluster_points <- X[clusters == cluster_ids[i], , drop = FALSE]
    centroids[i, ] <- colMeans(cluster_points)
    dispersions[i] <- mean(sqrt(rowSums((sweep(cluster_points, 2, centroids[i, ]))^2)))
  }
  
  # Calculate DB index
  DB <- 0
  for (i in 1:k) {
    max_ratio <- 0
    for (j in 1:k) {
      if (i != j) {
        d_ij <- sqrt(sum((centroids[i, ] - centroids[j, ])^2))
        ratio <- (dispersions[i] + dispersions[j]) / d_ij
        max_ratio <- max(max_ratio, ratio)
      }
    }
    DB <- DB + max_ratio
  }
  
  return(DB / k)
}

# ============================================
# 2. RUN THE ANALYSIS
# ============================================

# Run for all three methods
results_all <- find_optimal_k(X, k_range = 2:15, methods = c("vmf", "gmm", "fcm"))

# ============================================
# 3. ELBOW METHOD VISUALIZATION
# ============================================

# Normalize metrics to 0-1 scale for comparison
normalize_metric <- function(x, higher_better = TRUE) {
  if (higher_better) {
    (x - min(x, na.rm = TRUE)) / (max(x, na.rm = TRUE) - min(x, na.rm = TRUE))
  } else {
    1 - (x - min(x, na.rm = TRUE)) / (max(x, na.rm = TRUE) - min(x, na.rm = TRUE))
  }
}

# Create plotting data with normalized metrics
plot_results <- results_all %>%
  group_by(method) %>%
  mutate(
    silhouette_norm = normalize_metric(silhouette, TRUE),
    BIC_norm = normalize_metric(BIC, TRUE),  # Higher BIC is better
    AIC_norm = normalize_metric(AIC, TRUE),
    CH_norm = normalize_metric(calinski_harabasz, TRUE),
    DB_norm = normalize_metric(davies_bouldin, FALSE),  # Lower DB is better
    entropy_norm = normalize_metric(entropy, FALSE)
  )

# Plot 1: Silhouette
p1 <- ggplot(results_all, aes(x = k, y = silhouette, color = method, group = method)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Silhouette Score (Higher = Better)",
       x = "Number of Clusters (k)", y = "Silhouette Score") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(results_all$k))

# Plot 2: BIC (for methods that have it)
p2 <- ggplot(results_all %>% filter(!is.na(BIC)), 
             aes(x = k, y = BIC, color = method, group = method)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "BIC (Higher = Better)",
       x = "Number of Clusters (k)", y = "BIC") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(results_all$k))

# Plot 3: Calinski-Harabasz
p3 <- ggplot(results_all, aes(x = k, y = calinski_harabasz, color = method, group = method)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Calinski-Harabasz Index (Higher = Better)",
       x = "Number of Clusters (k)", y = "CH Index") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(results_all$k))

# Plot 4: Davies-Bouldin
p4 <- ggplot(results_all, aes(x = k, y = davies_bouldin, color = method, group = method)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Davies-Bouldin Index (Lower = Better)",
       x = "Number of Clusters (k)", y = "DB Index") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(results_all$k))

# Plot 5: Entropy
p5 <- ggplot(results_all, aes(x = k, y = entropy, color = method, group = method)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Entropy (Lower = Better)",
       x = "Number of Clusters (k)", y = "Entropy") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(results_all$k))

# Combined plot
library(gridExtra)
grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, ncol = 2)

# ============================================
# 4. COMPOSITE SCORE
# ============================================

# Calculate composite score (average of normalized metrics)
composite_scores <- plot_results %>%
  mutate(
    composite = (silhouette_norm + CH_norm + DB_norm + entropy_norm) / 4
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(k, method, composite, silhouette, calinski_harabasz, 
         davies_bouldin, entropy)

# Plot composite scores
p6 <- ggplot(composite_scores, aes(x = k, y = composite, color = method, group = method)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Composite Score (Higher = Better)",
       subtitle = "Average of normalized Silhouette, CH, DB, and Entropy",
       x = "Number of Clusters (k)", y = "Composite Score") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(composite_scores$k)) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p6)

# ============================================
# 5. IDENTIFY OPTIMAL K
# ============================================

# Best k by each metric
optimal_k_summary <- results_all %>%
  group_by(method) %>%
  summarise(
    k_by_silhouette = k[which.max(silhouette)],
    k_by_BIC = k[which.max(BIC)],
    k_by_CH = k[which.max(calinski_harabasz)],
    k_by_DB = k[which.min(davies_bouldin)],
    k_by_entropy = k[which.min(entropy)]
  )

cat("\n=== OPTIMAL K BY METRIC ===\n")
print(optimal_k_summary)

# Best k by composite score
best_composite <- composite_scores %>%
  group_by(method) %>%
  slice_max(composite, n = 1)

cat("\n=== OPTIMAL K BY COMPOSITE SCORE ===\n")
print(best_composite)

# ============================================
# 6. ELBOW DETECTION (AUTOMATIC)
# ============================================

find_elbow <- function(x, y) {
  # Find elbow using maximum distance from line
  n <- length(x)
  p1 <- c(x[1], y[1])
  p2 <- c(x[n], y[n])
  
  distances <- numeric(n)
  for (i in 1:n) {
    p <- c(x[i], y[i])
    # Distance from point to line
    distances[i] <- abs((p2[2] - p1[2]) * p[1] - (p2[1] - p1[1]) * p[2] + 
                          p2[1] * p1[2] - p2[2] * p1[1]) /
      sqrt((p2[2] - p1[2])^2 + (p2[1] - p1[1])^2)
  }
  
  return(x[which.max(distances)])
}

# Detect elbows
elbow_results <- results_all %>%
  group_by(method) %>%
  summarise(
    k_elbow_silhouette = find_elbow(k, silhouette),
    k_elbow_CH = find_elbow(k, calinski_harabasz),
    k_elbow_BIC = find_elbow(k, ifelse(is.na(BIC), 0, BIC))
  )

cat("\n=== ELBOW DETECTION ===\n")
print(elbow_results)

# ============================================
# 7. GAP STATISTIC (Gold Standard)
# ============================================

library(cluster)

gap_stat_vmf <- function(X, K.max = 15, B = 50) {
  # Custom gap statistic for vMF
  X_normalized <- X / sqrt(rowSums(X^2))
  n <- nrow(X)
  
  W_k <- numeric(K.max)
  W_kb <- matrix(0, nrow = K.max, ncol = B)
  
  for (k in 1:K.max) {
    cat(sprintf("Gap statistic: k = %d\n", k))
    
    # Real data
    vmf <- movMF(X, k = k, nruns = 5)
    clusters <- predict(vmf, X)
    
    # Within-cluster dispersion (using cosine distance)
    W_k[k] <- 0
    for (i in unique(clusters)) {
      cluster_points <- X_normalized[clusters == i, , drop = FALSE]
      if (nrow(cluster_points) > 1) {
        D <- as.matrix(dist(cluster_points, method = "euclidean"))
        W_k[k] <- W_k[k] + sum(D^2) / (2 * nrow(cluster_points))
      }
    }
    
    # Reference distribution (uniform on sphere)
    for (b in 1:B) {
      # Generate random points on unit sphere
      X_ref <- matrix(rnorm(n * ncol(X)), nrow = n)
      X_ref <- X_ref / sqrt(rowSums(X_ref^2))
      
      vmf_ref <- movMF(X_ref, k = k, nruns = 3)
      clusters_ref <- predict(vmf_ref, X_ref)
      
      W_kb[k, b] <- 0
      for (i in unique(clusters_ref)) {
        cluster_points <- X_ref[clusters_ref == i, , drop = FALSE]
        if (nrow(cluster_points) > 1) {
          D <- as.matrix(dist(cluster_points, method = "euclidean"))
          W_kb[k, b] <- W_kb[k, b] + sum(D^2) / (2 * nrow(cluster_points))
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Calculate gap statistic
  log_W_k <- log(W_k)
  log_W_kb <- log(W_kb)
  gap <- rowMeans(log_W_kb) - log_W_k
  se <- apply(log_W_kb, 1, sd) * sqrt(1 + 1/B)
  
  # Find optimal k (first k where gap(k) >= gap(k+1) - se(k+1))
  optimal_k <- 1
  for (k in 1:(K.max - 1)) {
    if (gap[k] >= gap[k + 1] - se[k + 1]) {
      optimal_k <- k
      break
    }
  }
  
  list(
    gap = gap,
    se = se,
    optimal_k = optimal_k,
    gap_data = data.frame(k = 1:K.max, gap = gap, se = se)
  )
}

# Run gap statistic (WARNING: This is slow!)
cat("\nRunning Gap Statistic (this may take a while)...\n")
gap_result <- gap_stat_vmf(X, K.max = 10, B = 20)  # Reduced B for speed

# Plot gap statistic
p7 <- ggplot(gap_result$gap_data, aes(x = k, y = gap)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = gap - se, ymax = gap + se), width = 0.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = gap_result$optimal_k, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Gap Statistic",
       subtitle = sprintf("Optimal k = %d", gap_result$optimal_k),
       x = "Number of Clusters (k)", y = "Gap Statistic") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = gap_result$gap_data$k)

print(p7)

# ============================================
# 8. FINAL RECOMMENDATION
# ============================================

cat("\n=== FINAL RECOMMENDATION ===\n")
cat(sprintf("Gap Statistic suggests: k = %d\n", gap_result$optimal_k))
cat("\nConsensus from multiple metrics:\n")
print(best_composite)

# # Save all results
# write.csv(results_all, "k_selection_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)
# write.csv(optimal_k_summary, "optimal_k_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)
# 
# cat("\nResults saved to CSV files.\n")
```


```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(movMF)
library(mclust)
library(cluster)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(e1071) # Required for cmeans/FCM
library(tidyr) # Required for pivot_longer

# ============================================
# 1. FUNCTION TO COLLECT MEMBERSHIP INFO
# ============================================

#' Retrieves and combines the membership (soft-assignment) probabilities 
#' from vMF, GMM, and FCM models for a given number of clusters (k).
#'
#' @param X The input data matrix of embeddings (rows are observations, columns are dimensions).
#' @param k The optimal number of clusters to use for the models.
#' @param methods A character vector of models to run (default: c("vmf", "gmm", "fcm")).
#' @return A long-format data frame containing Embedding_ID, Method, Cluster, and Membership_Value.
get_optimal_k_memberships <- function(X, k, methods = c("vmf", "gmm", "fcm")) {

  # Check for k validity
  if (k < 2) stop("k must be greater than or equal to 2.")

  cat(sprintf("Running clustering models for k = %d...\n", k))

  # Normalize data (crucial for vMF and standard practice for text embeddings)
  X_normalized <- X / sqrt(rowSums(X^2))

  all_memberships <- list()
  n_obs <- nrow(X)
  
  # ---------------------
  # vMF Mixture Model (movMF)
  # ---------------------
  if ("vmf" %in% methods) {
    cat("Fitting vMF...\n")
    tryCatch({
      # Run movMF on the original (un-normalized) data, as movMF handles normalization internally
      vmf_model <- movMF(X, k = k, nruns = 10, control = list(maxiter = 100))
      vmf_probs <- predict(vmf_model, X, type = "memberships")
      
      # Convert membership matrix to long format using tidyr::pivot_longer
      vmf_df <- as.data.frame(vmf_probs)
      names(vmf_df) <- paste0("vMF_C", 1:k)
      vmf_df$Embedding_ID <- 1:n_obs
      
      all_memberships[['vMF']] <- vmf_df %>%
        tidyr::pivot_longer(
          cols = starts_with("vMF_C"),
          names_to = "Cluster",
          values_to = "Membership_Value"
        ) %>%
        mutate(
          Method = "vMF",
          Cluster = as.integer(gsub("vMF_C", "", Cluster))
        )
      
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat(paste("Error running vMF (k=", k, "):", e$message, "\n"))
    })
  }

  # ---------------------
  # GMM (Mclust)
  # ---------------------
  if ("gmm" %in% methods) {
    cat("Fitting GMM...\n")
    tryCatch({
      # Run Mclust on the normalized data
      gmm_model <- Mclust(X_normalized, G = k, modelNames = "VVI", verbose = FALSE)
      gmm_probs <- gmm_model$z # $z contains the responsibilities (probabilities)
      
      gmm_df <- as.data.frame(gmm_probs)
      names(gmm_df) <- paste0("GMM_C", 1:k)
      gmm_df$Embedding_ID <- 1:n_obs
      
      all_memberships[['GMM']] <- gmm_df %>%
        tidyr::pivot_longer(
          cols = starts_with("GMM_C"),
          names_to = "Cluster",
          values_to = "Membership_Value"
        ) %>%
        mutate(
          Method = "GMM",
          Cluster = as.integer(gsub("GMM_C", "", Cluster))
        )
      
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat(paste("Error running GMM (k=", k, "):", e$message, "\n"))
    })
  }

  # ---------------------
  # Fuzzy C-Means (FCM)
  # ---------------------
  if ("fcm" %in% methods) {
    cat("Fitting FCM...\n")
    tryCatch({
      # Run cmeans on the normalized data
      fcm_model <- e1071::cmeans(X_normalized, centers = k, iter.max = 100, m = 2)
      fcm_probs <- fcm_model$membership
      
      fcm_df <- as.data.frame(fcm_probs)
      names(fcm_df) <- paste0("FCM_C", 1:k)
      fcm_df$Embedding_ID <- 1:n_obs
      
      all_memberships[['FCM']] <- fcm_df %>%
        tidyr::pivot_longer(
          cols = starts_with("FCM_C"),
          names_to = "Cluster",
          values_to = "Membership_Value"
        ) %>%
        mutate(
          Method = "FCM",
          Cluster = as.integer(gsub("FCM_C", "", Cluster))
        )
      
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat(paste("Error running FCM (k=", k, "):", e$message, "\n"))
    })
  }
  
  # Combine all results into one comprehensive table
  if (length(all_memberships) > 0) {
    final_table <- bind_rows(all_memberships)
    cat("\nClustering complete. Returning combined membership table.\n")
    return(final_table)
  } else {
    warning("No clustering methods were successfully executed. Returning NULL.")
    return(NULL)
  }
}

# ============================================
# 2. FUNCTION FOR HARD ASSIGNMENTS
# ============================================

#' Subsets the long-format membership table to include only the most-likely 
#' cluster (hard assignment) for each embedding and method.
#'
#' @param membership_df The long-format data frame generated by 
#'   get_optimal_k_memberships.
#' @return A data frame containing Embedding_ID, Method, Cluster (the hard 
#'   assignment), and the corresponding Membership_Value.
get_hard_assignments <- function(membership_df) {
  
  if (is.null(membership_df) || nrow(membership_df) == 0) {
    warning("Input membership data frame is empty or NULL.")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Group by Embedding and Method, then select the row with the maximum 
  # Membership_Value (i.e., the most likely cluster).
  hard_assignments <- membership_df %>%
    group_by(Embedding_ID, Method) %>%
    # slice_max is used for convenience to pick the maximum value.
    # We use n=1 to ensure only one cluster is picked per group.
    slice_max(Membership_Value, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::select(Embedding_ID, Method, Cluster, Max_Membership_Value = Membership_Value)
  
  return(hard_assignments)
}


# ============================================
# 3. EXECUTION EXAMPLE
# ============================================

# --- SETUP DUMMY DATA ---
# In a real scenario, X would be loaded from your data source.
# Creating a sample matrix X (200 documents, 10-dimensional embeddings)
set.seed(42)
X <- matrix(rnorm(200 * 10), nrow = 200) 

# --- DEFINE OPTIMAL K ---
# Replace 5 with the optimal k value determined from your analysis (e.g., k_by_CH, best_composite)
K_OPT <- 5

# --- GET MEMBERSHIP TABLE (SOFT ASSIGNMENTS) ---
membership_table <- get_optimal_k_memberships(X, k = K_OPT)

# --- GET HARD ASSIGNMENTS ---
hard_assignments_table <- NULL
if (!is.null(membership_table)) {
  hard_assignments_table <- get_hard_assignments(membership_table)
}


# ============================================
# 4. DISPLAY RESULTS & VISUALIZATION
# ============================================

if (!is.null(membership_table)) {
  
  cat("\n=== Combined Membership Table (First 15 Rows) for Optimal k =", K_OPT, "===\n")
  print(head(membership_table, 15)) 
  
  if (!is.null(hard_assignments_table)) {
     cat("\n=== Hard Assignments Table (First 15 Rows) ===\n")
     cat("This table shows the single most-likely cluster for each embedding and method.\n")
     print(head(hard_assignments_table, 15))
  }


  # Visualization Example: Compare the soft assignments for the first embedding
  embedding_1_memberships <- membership_table %>%
    filter(Embedding_ID == 1) %>%
    mutate(
      Cluster_ID = factor(paste0("C", Cluster))
    )

  p_membership <- ggplot(embedding_1_memberships, aes(x = Cluster_ID, y = Membership_Value, fill = Method)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
    facet_wrap(~ Method) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Membership Probabilities for Embedding ID 1 (k =", K_OPT, ")"),
      subtitle = "Comparing vMF, GMM, and FCM soft assignments",
      x = "Cluster ID",
      y = "Membership / Probability"
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("vMF" = "#3B82F6", "GMM" = "#10B981", "FCM" = "#F59E0B")) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")

  # Print the visualization
  print(p_membership)
}
```

```{r}
X <- embeddings
membership_table <- get_optimal_k_memberships(X, k = 10)
hard_assignments_table <- get_hard_assignments(membership_table)
```

```{r}
summary <- hard_assignments_table %>% 
    filter(Method == "GMM") %>%   # only results from GMM
    mutate(Column_Name = rownames(X)) %>%   # add the source column name for each embedding
    group_by(Cluster) %>% 
    summarise(min = min(Max_Membership_Value),   # minimum likelihood of an embedding in a given group/cluster
              n = n(),   # the number of embeddings in each group/cluster
              columns = paste(Column_Name, collapse = ";;"))   # column names for each group/cluster

summary
```

```{r}
write.csv(summary, "~/OmicsMLRepo/MetaHarmonizer/EDA/data/GMM_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
